data.description_suggestion: Creates a new webhook configuration for porting events. Accepts webhook URL, event types, and authentication details in the request body. Returns the created webhook configuration resource with a unique identifier. This configuration will receive event notifications for port-in request status changes and related porting activities.

data.current_summary: Create a bulk eligibility check for a set of numbers that you want to host in Twilio.
data.current_description: Create a bulk eligibility check for a set of numbers that you want to host in Twilio.
data.summary_suggestion: Create bulk eligibility check for hosted numbers
data.description_suggestion: Initiates a bulk eligibility check for multiple phone numbers intended for Twilio hosting. Accepts a list of phone numbers and returns a request ID (202 Accepted) for asynchronous processing. Use the request ID to poll for results via FetchBulkEligibility. This operation validates whether the numbers can be hosted on Twilio's platform.
data.reasoning: Original description lacks input details (what parameters are needed), output details (what is returned, including the request ID for polling), and side effects (asynchronous processing). Added clarity about the async nature and how to retrieve results, which is critical for AI agent understanding.

data.current_summary: Allows to create a new port in request
data.current_description: Allows to create a new port in request
data.summary_suggestion: Create a phone number port-in request

data.current_summary: Allows to cancel a port in request by SID
data.current_description: Allows to cancel a port in request by SID
data.description_suggestion: Cancels an active port-in request identified by PortInRequestSid. This operation terminates the porting process and prevents the phone numbers from being transferred. Use this to abort a port-in request before completion. Note: Cancellation may not be possible if the port-in has already progressed to certain stages.
